reviews.review_description: This is one of the best little shorts I've ever watched.

Adorably cute, extremely short movie that your young kids would love! (I enjoyed it even as an adult without children.) It's bright and colorful and all the good things about VR graphics, and just VR in general. 

It's just a few minutes long and I certainly didn't expect my emotional reaction at the end 🥹
This isn’t a game, it’s a movie—and easily one of the cutest VR films you’ll ever watch!  Kudos to Oculus Studio for creating something so charming and absolutely deserving of its Emmy award!!!
I liked it wish it was longer and had similar things like it
I don't what is wrong with the haters, this is a very good short movie, although it said it was a game
Honestly wholesome and it made me so happy
It is so cute🥳❤️
Made a white tear run down my leg 🌹🌹🫀🫀🫀🤧🤧
Oh my goodness I wish this was longer! I didn’t want it to end!! The cutest story ever. Nearly made me cry. Highly HIGHLY recommend.
Giving quest 3 owners this piece of garbage compared to the rift version is pathetic.
I loved this short film but i just wish it was like an hour like an actual movie
even though it’s not a game, it’s fine. It’s a cute movie and I love it. You should definitely play it or buy it whatever the term.
It’s not a game. It’s a cute movie
The App Store said this was a game but it turns out it was just a movie

My nephew for the most part is nonverbal. You have to heavily prompt him for him to say things to you. But after he watched this video he started asking for me auntie repeatedly. Look me in my eyes and grabbed my hand. I knew that he must have really enjoyed it in order for him to speak to me that directly. Thank you for making this experience possible for him!
Just a video
This game has to be 1m out of 10

when I watch this animation, I was about to cry because how sad it was you see back in kindergarten I didn’t have any friends but in my birthday I made a wish that I would’ve made friends and I actually did. This is super sad and good. I rate this game a 10 out of 10
It’s just a movie like I thought it was game.
SO BAD ! SO BORING! SO SKIBBIDIIIIIIIIIII!
It’s not even a game it’s just a dumb story. I give it 0 stars
IF YOU PLAY THIS ON YOUR VR DO NOT LOOK AT THE FLOOR I DID THIS AND NOW I FEEL SICK :(

Please restore the realtime 3D version that was narrated by Elijah Wood.
The Quest 3 can handle it.

This is just a 360 video that someone recorded from the actual app.

The second I booted up the app I knew I was gonna be thrown into a s***storm  I soon noticed it uses 3dof tracking and is a prerequisite rendered video, which isn't good, not even for a free video the days of GearVR are long gone, what happened to the real time 3d version
Henry
It is the worst app in the whole world 🌎 I hate it!🤬🤬🤬

reviews.review_title: Once a great app, now a shell of itself
reviews.review_description: “Henry” was one of my first wow moments in VR. I first watched this on Oculus Rift and then again on Quest 1. The original app was made in Unreal with full 6DoF which allowed the viewer to move around Henry’s house. The visuals were so crisp because it was all in-engine. It baffles me that Meta has removed this version of the app and replaced it with just a video file. It may be a hi-res VR video, but definitely not as immersive as the Unreal Engine version and no longer 6DoF. In the original version, no matter where you were standing, Henry would occasionally turn to you and make eye contact. So it made it feel like you were there. The new version just has him look into camera.   Also, Elijah Wood was the narrator and he has now been replaced by someone else for some reason. RIP Oculus Story Studio - Meta shut that team down too soon.
My 4 and 6 year old loved it. I always play this when they start bugging me about playing video games.
My grandson that have  autism loves this story and watch it regularly. Make more please
henry
Loved it!

I've been playing FPS  games since I was conceived and this has got to be the ABSOLUTE WORST FPS I'VE EVER SEEN first off the graphics? dog crap I've never seen anything that looks more like a game from the 1800s than this well surely the mechanics and community redeem this game? nope mechanics overall feel like flying a plane while being quadriplegic and don't even get me STARTED on the community never more have I been called slurs derogatory terms and things I've never heard of and I played call of duty MW2 in its prime
Henry is a quick and cute story that is really well made. Adults and kids will have as much fun and the message is nice at the end. A must have for beginners with VR.
This vr game is amazing. The message in it was so accurate to me I think people should make more of these😊
Great short story with an amazing message. No wonder it won an Emmy!
Short but sweet vr video

I've Never written a review but this time I will not only write one because the story focused on a birthday, in which I'm celebrating this month, but because it's beautiful and entertaining and sure it's short but it's totally free!! It's way better than some that you actually pay and did I mention it is beautiful! It's a short story but immersive like the Avatar movie, finally some one is on the right path of using the VR to make a movie I would even pay money to have if it was at least an hour long. People pay over 30 dollars for a Blu-ray why not make a full length on this type of quality...great job.
This little episode was super cute! Kids would love it! I only wish things like this on the Oculus were longer.

ABSOLUTE BANGER OF AN EXPERIENCE I LAUGHED I CRYED AND MOST OF ALL I FELT SO IMMERSED IN THIS WONDERFUL AND OUTSTANDING EXPERIENCE YOU MUST TRY IT FOR YOUR SELF. HENRY IS MY NUMBER ONE FAVOURITE EXPERIENCE OF ALL TIME NOTHING CAN BEAT THIS BEAST OF AN APPLICATION. don’t believe try it for yourself.
This was super cute. I loved the animation and the story. Delightful.

This virtual experience was better then any I've seen before it had romance horror but the ending was a true masterpiece. I mean who doesn't love Henry so wholesome. 
P.S frick you jack ruined my chances 🤣
It is immersive, cute and completely free short animated movie that will amuse you and the rest of your family. Not something you would like to keep but that is not a flaw. Thanks!

I loved watching every minute of this, then character of Henry is so genuine you can't help but instantly feel for him.
Laughed, felt sad for Henry and felt proper heartfelt joy at the end too.
Let my Father watch it as well, he normally doesn't enjoy VR but even he thoroughly enjoyed it.
It’s a series of cartoon movies, it’s really interesting and warm heart, I think you’d better share it with your children, they must be love it! Oh, is QUEST VR only for people over 13 years old?
Great job guys! Thanks a lot! Our 2 year old watches this every night with much enthusiasm:)
Great short film, especially for the younger ones as no movement or control is needed.

The original was not a prerendered movie. I was actually able to walk around and be immersed in the story. Now the whole world moves with you like an old cellphone VR experience. Very disappointing. Love the story but very lacking. Bummer for sure! Devs, do it right please! It is worth the effort. Please fix it.
really short but cute

While it's a pretty much a one watch and done sort of experience, this is a great app to get if you're likely to demonstrate your headset to kids. A good suggestion is have them sit on the floor to watch it. The trigger button pauses/play while the thumbstick fast forwards/rewinds through scenes.

Unlike on the Rift, Henry for the Go and Quest is 360 video recording of the Henry experience that looks like it's in 5k resolution. I'm pretty sure it's using John Carmack's modified viewer though I don't know which one. The one weird artifact is the floor under you looking like a depression. Beyond that, you're seeing what only Rift users with beefy computers would see.
Pretendered, so no 6DOF. This makes this experience very awkward and uncomfortable. The original on the Rift I would recommend a lot more than this.

This isn't an app, it's a pre-rendered video. And if you move your head the world sways around you. And if you tilt your head you get serious eye strain and motion sickness.  Real nausea that you thought you were completely immune to as a VR user.

And the story is designed for very young children or toddlers, and there are good arguments to say that children that young shouldn't be dissociating what they see from the rest of their senses, so VR seems an odd choice for this particular story.

So:

*Quality of animation*: Great, sure.  It would make a fine TV episode.  I see my two year old neice enjoying things like this a lot.  I skipped through bits but liked the glowing balloons.

*Appropriateness of the concept*: Poor.  If it's for VR, need to aim at children at least grade-school age or higher (or with the pedigree of the makers involved, aim higher still!).

*Experience of watching this format of thing in a Quest*: Very poor.  Some adaptation or innovation is needed to make this work.  It is not comfortable.
Incredible story! The VR really suited it! We NEED A sequel!

reviews.review_title: You get what you pay for
reviews.review_description:
For what it’s worth this is a good story, short, but a good one, something you’d expect to see in one of those animated shorts on YouTube. Being in vr doesn’t really contribute much to the story telling other than making you feel like you’re really there.

However a big drawback is how this is only 3dof, if you’re prone to motion sickness skip this one

I'm left wondering if I saw a different short film than the other reviewers?  I thought this was so well done and while very short, it was so fun!  I felt like I had entered a world much like Winnie the Pooh lived in. The animation was beautiful and even at age 50, I found myself laughing and really enjoying the story.  I'm someone who gets motion sickness a lot in VR experiences, but I didn't even slightly get it here.  My only disappointment was that it ended so soon but at the price of free, I certainly can't complain about that!  Thank you to the devs for this experience.  I can't wait to show my 10 and 11 year olds tomorrow!  More like this, please!

This isn’t an app but a short cartoon about 5 min long. There’s no interaction. That being said it’s a delightful story. 

It’s free. Download it and check it out. Kids will especially enjoy it.
It was okay on GearVR. Now on Quest, it is dated and disappointing they would release this again. Also can I say the story is quite sad and doesnt redeem itself by the end.
Awsome story with great animation. Would not recommend to moms and emotional people. I’m a 12 years old and almost cried.
nice and cute animation, but quality like 30 fps in the white colors of frames(((
Bad you don’t do anything

reviews.review_title: Cute, great graphics, free! One bug.
reviews.review_description:
I immediately noticed a bug. It doesn't handle you tilting your head to the side. So you have to keep your eyes level.

Other than that, it's cute and charming and is a great demo of the graphical capabilities of the quest.
Pointless ,

reviews.review_title: Perfect short animation movie for free
reviews.review_description:
It is just unbelievable to be able to watch such an awesome movie for free ! Open to every ages, young as old, just sit on the floor leg crossed if you're not a kid to avoid distorsion.
It's a short expérience but insanely qualitative

reviews.review_title: A cute story with solid animation
reviews.review_description:
This was one of my earlier VR experiences,  and it proved to be a great choice for that. The story is sweet and inspiring in a light hearted way, and the animation is great. There is no movement, so it's a good experience for new VR adopters. 

I did find it limited in what it did, there was no innovation in it whatsoever, but it's easy to enjoy. It felt very much like what would happen if Disney started making VR content (🤞).
A nice, short story!
Very nice cartoon with interesting effects

reviews.review_title: This is adorable wish it was longer
reviews.review_description: Needed to be longer I want more stories and voices for the characters including the turtle

This was a nice little story but really not particularly interesting. I got a very lukewarm reception from the kids after they had watched it. I watched it myself and got quite a bit of motion sickness from it, which was odd as you don' move about at all.
This game is cool and all but it was really not that good I thought the quality was bad it was really blurry yeah I just don’t like the game
Sorry story of a hedgehog whoo wants to hug everyone. Non interactive and kind of predictable for adults but really fun to watch for younger audiences.

My kids loved the story. Wish more of these were around for the quest. 

These stories would be great for kids instead of it mainly for games
This video it’s heartwarming at the end, at the beginning it’s kinda sad. I like how it tells you that you can find friendships even if your different.

So i can pause, unpause, and skip forward and back.
If i skip to the end, it starts over.

Maybe I just missed something, but how do you exit this thing?
Quality video, sweet character is definitely worth watching it.

I have a pet hedgehog, so was obligated to watch this. It’s very cute, but hope this doesn’t make some kids want a pet hedgehog, expecting them to be anything like Henry. Hedgehogs are solitary animals, and although my hedgehog is more friendly and sociable than most from my understanding, they make terrible pets for kids. Overall, I really liked how the balloon dogs danced in front of you, but wish the room didn’t move with you.
So cute for ages and I was a little bit scared of the dark. But it was little funny cute though
Omg this was so cute and great I suggest everyone watch it ‘ all ages

Not sure why people are writing a bad review. It’s free so if you don’t like it remove it. It’s a short video on Henry the hedgehog that has issues with friendship. 5 minutes long roughly. I downloaded to show my niece something nice and fun. To make it better there could be more short stories. Enjoy

This is a beautiful story that is very well written. It uses the VR capabilities very well and allows you to look 360 degrees at all times. I watched it and loved it. I then made my mom watch it and she loved it. Same with my dad and sister. Everyone I have showed it to agrees that it is a great movie.
Everyone needs to see this.

reviews.review_title: I did not see it yet
reviews.review_description: It seems cool tho

reviews.review_title: cute story and helped me get used to VR
reviews.review_description: I was really uncomfortable when I turned on the VR and all the objects were very close. So I watch this one to help me get used to the feeling of VR and it helped immensely! The story is really cute, too! I saw some negative reviews where you couldn't pick up things or move around, and I don't think that is the purpose of this one. I think you're supposed to stay still and watch a story. thanks for helping me get my VR legs!￼
